journey.title: BCA — foundations
journey.text: Started Bachelor of Computer Application (2021–2024). Python stopped being a subject and became a way to turn messy work into systems.

journey.title: Global Tenders — scraping at scale
journey.text: First professional chapter as a Python developer: Selenium pipelines, OpenCV/Tesseract for captcha-heavy flows, and tools that made content teams faster.

journey.title: Larsen & Toubro PESIc — AI into operations
journey.text: Moved from automation into production AI: a RAG ticketing chatbot with a fine-tuned 7B model, FAISS retrieval, WebSockets, and ML for resolution-time prediction.

journey.title: Parag Parikh — wealth systems + RAG
journey.text: As Assistant Manager – Python Developer, building Account Aggregator wealth dashboards, conversational finance Q&A with Qwen-13B + FAISS, and ops platforms for inventory and distribution.

lessons: Production RAG is less about demos and more about grounding, latency, and trust.
Automation that saves hours beats clever code that nobody runs.
Clear consent and audit trails matter as much as model quality in financial systems.
Trekking teaches the same discipline as shipping: pace, preparation, turn-back judgement.
Document the evaluation loop — feedback from real users is the best dataset.
beliefs: AI should answer from data, not invent confidence.
Backend craft is quiet reliability under load.
Personality belongs beside the résumé — trails and yatras shape how I build.
Ship vertical slices: consent → data → insight → conversation.
howIWork: Start from the user question and the source of truth.
Prefer FastAPI/Django for critical paths; keep the stack boring where it must not fail.
Measure retrieval quality before tuning prompts.
Build admin panels early — operators need visibility.
Protect deep work; weekends on trails reset judgement.

workflow.detail: Prioritize production issues, then one AI/backend vertical slice.
API contracts, retrieval tests, UI for the path a real user takes.
Check grounding, security of tokens/consent, and failure modes.
Trek, shrine, or a long walk — then return with clearer systems thinking.

goals: Deepen production RAG and LLM fine-tuning for domain workflows.
Keep shipping FastAPI systems that stay calm under concurrent users.
Complete the twelfth Jyotirlinga — Ramanathaswamy, Rameswaram.
Document treks and engineering lessons in the same honest voice.
vision: A career where enterprise AI systems and Himalayan/Sahyadri trails reinforce each other — precise software, durable judgement, and a life that still moves outdoors.

books.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
books.author: Martin Kleppmann
books.note: How reliable systems think — still the reference when pipelines grow.

books.title: Hands-On Machine Learning
books.author: Aurélien Géron
books.note: Practical ML intuition behind models we still ship.

books.title: Atomic Habits
books.author: James Clear
books.note: Compounding applies to commits and trekking mileage alike.

workspace: Mumbai base — hybrid work with deep-focus blocks
FastAPI + notebook experiments side by side
Notebook for architecture sketches before code
Weekend packing list never far from the desk
